content-right-description1:
Overview

Satellites are absolutly essential for the whole game, collecting data of planets.
You wil require knowledge about:
  • rockets
content-right-description2:
Step 1: Assembeling the satellite

First of you will need to assemble your satellite, for this you can use:

  • satellite (required)
  • batteries (required)
  • solar panels (optional)
  • lora module (optional)
  • radiation shield (optional)
  • data storage (required)
  • optical telescope (optional)
  • composition scanner (optional)
  • mass scanner (optional)
Batteries are required to give the satellite power, if you are close to a star, you will be able to use solar panels, if you go further away you will require more batteries, which can be precharged.
Any data collection will require 100rf, if power runs out the sattelite will turn off until there is enough power for collection.

Every tick there is an 1 in 10000 chance for radiation damage for your sattelite, to protect your satellite you will require a radiation shield.

Data is on default stored in your data storage, via a lora module you will be able to transfer the data back to the ground for distanced above 0.3AU, if you dont use a lora module for an distance above 0.3AU you will need to return the satellite with a rocket.

Depending on what data you want to get you will need to use eather the mass scanner (for mass), the optical telescope (for distance) or the composition scanner (for composition).

After palcing all the items and a satellite id chip, which will be your connection to the satellite, you have to press build, which will burn your id chip and give you the finsihed sattelite (by reinputing the satellite and clicking 'build' again you can modify/disassemble your satellite).
Don't lose your chip since you cant get it again, copying can be done by placing a second id chip left of the to be copied one and clicking 'copy chip'.
content-right-description3:
Step 2: launching the satellite

Next up you will need to send the satellite into the right orbit on a rocket, the satellites you wanna launch will needd to be placed into a cargo hold.
Launching can be done via a satellite launch station, in wich you will need to input the planet id chip of the target planet, then clicking 'launch' will launch your rocket and return it back to you without the satellite.
You will also be able to see the ETA (estimated arrival time) for your satellite.
content-right-description4:
Step 3.1: receiving data (lora module)

If you installed a lora module or have the sattelite within 0.3 AU of distance you can use a satellite monitor, which will require power. In the right slot you will need to place your satellite id chip which will display it's current instrument, its orbit, power level and data storage.
by clicking 'download data' the data will slowly be transfered into the left collumn.
To output power you will need to eather place a data storage in the top left slot or use a wireless data transfer block on the block.
content-right-description5:
Step 3.2: receiving data (returning the satellite)

If you didnt use a lora module and are more than 0.3 AU away from your sattelite or just want to retreive your satellite, you can use the satellite launch station, but now instead if inputing a planet id chip, you input your sattelite id chip and press 'launch'.
This will launch the rocket and return the satellite in it's cargo hold.
To retreive your data, place the satellite back into the satellite assembler and take your data storage out of it.
